task: check the landing page title tag and suggest if it could be improved for SEO

assignments.content: Check the current <title> tag on the landing page (src/indiestack/routes/landing.py) and evaluate it for SEO best practices — keyword placement, length (50-60 chars), clarity, and click-through appeal. Suggest improved alternatives if the current title could be better optimized for search queries like 'developer tools', 'indie dev tools', 'AI coding tools' etc.

decomposition.reasoning: This is a straightforward SEO audit task. Only the Content/SEO department is needed — they'll read the landing page route file to find the current title tag, evaluate it against SEO best practices, and suggest improvements. No code changes, no backend work, no deployment needed.

strategy_review.reasoning: Title tag optimization is one of the highest ROI SEO tasks — 5 minutes of work, permanent impact on organic CTR. With ~53 users and no paid acquisition budget, organic search is a critical growth channel. Zero risk (it's a review + suggestion, not even a deploy). The content department is the right owner for copywriting/SEO work.

approved_tasks.content: Check the current <title> tag on the landing page (src/indiestack/routes/landing.py) and evaluate it for SEO best practices — keyword placement, length (50-60 chars), clarity, and click-through appeal. Suggest improved alternatives if the current title could be better optimized for search queries like 'developer tools', 'indie dev tools', 'AI coding tools' etc.

conditions: Validate target keywords against IndieStack's actual positioning — 'AI coding tools' may be misleading since IndieStack is a discovery layer FOR tools, not a coding tool itself. Better targets might be 'find developer tools', 'developer tool discovery', or 'best indie developer tools'.
Keep the suggestion as a recommendation for Patrick to approve — don't auto-deploy a title change without sign-off.
